evals.description: Scan a site — verifies all output sections populated with correct schema
assertions: Returns valid JSON without a top-level error field
url field matches the input url
timestamp is present in ISO8601 format
version_exposure object contains html_generator_tag, readme_html, rss_generator, and asset_version_hints fields
Each version_exposure field has found or accessible, version, and severity sub-fields
endpoint_exposure object contains wp_login, xmlrpc, rest_api_root, user_enumeration, and wp_admin_redirect fields
Each endpoint_exposure field has an accessible (or redirects_to_login) field and a severity field
security_headers object contains strict_transport_security, content_security_policy, x_content_type_options, x_frame_options, referrer_policy, permissions_policy, and x_xss_protection fields
Each security header has present, value (or null), and status fields
status in each security header is one of 'pass', 'fail', or 'warn'
ssl object contains https_in_use, http_redirects_to_https, mixed_content_detected, and hsts_preload_eligible fields
summary object contains critical, high, medium, low, info, and passed arrays
Output contains no recommendations or prose — findings only

evals.description: Site with exposed endpoints — xmlrpc accessible and user enumeration possible
assertions: Returns valid JSON without a top-level error field
If xmlrpc.php is accessible (HTTP 200 with XML body), endpoint_exposure.xmlrpc.accessible is true and severity is 'high'
If /wp-json/wp/v2/users returns HTTP 200 with array body, user_enumeration.accessible is true and usernames_found is a non-empty array
If readme.html returns HTTP 200, version_exposure.readme_html.accessible is true
High severity findings appear in summary.high array
Output does not include remediation recommendations — summary arrays contain factual findings only
Returns JSON with an error field
error value is 'site_unreachable' or similar
url field is present in the error response
Does not crash or return empty response
